key_insight: The paper extends shape optimization to thermoelasticity with temperature-dependent material properties, deriving shape derivatives for domain-integral functionals.
engineering_relevance: This enables more realistic thermal-structural design of components where stiffness and stress responses change with temperature.
method: Proves existence of the shape derivative for thermoelastic systems
Derives a generic expression for domain-integral objective functionals
Uses finite elements for PDE solves and a level set method for shape representation
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical results for two benchmark optimization settings: compliance minimization with a volume constraint and volume minimization with an L2 von Mises stress constraint.
watch_item: Results depend on accurate modeling of temperature-dependent constitutive laws and can be sensitive to level-set update and boundary-value discretization choices.
paper_title: Shape optimization for thermoelasticity with temperature-dependent material parameters
